Nova user-need hypotheses (testable, from README + code inspection 2026-05-01):
1. Agents and humans need persistent async message threads that survive process boundaries — testable: does a message sent by lotus persist after nova-server restart? (currently NO — InMemoryStore).
2. Orokin multi-agent pipelines need a way to branch conversations to new agents without rewriting message provenance — testable: does BranchThread preserve from/to fields? (YES — confirmed in code + CHANGELOG fix).
3. launchagent dispatch is fire-and-forget; nova would provide a reply channel so agents can return results to human:simon asynchronously — testable: can human:simon run 'nova inbox human:simon' and see a reply from lotus after dispatch? (currently only if both CLI sessions share in-process engine — NOT via daemon).

MISREAD USER-NEED [attacks 019de3ba-7b82-7240-8e7f-2a8a87a1ad11 hyp-3]: Research assumes Simon will run 'nova inbox human:simon' to receive agent replies. Simon has ADHD — poll-driven inbox is the exact UX anti-pattern for his neurodivergence. Actual need: interrupt-driven, results surface where he is already working (ordis/terminal). Nova delivers a second place Simon must remember to check; it does not reduce retrieval friction, it creates a new one.

MISREAD USER-NEED [attacks 019de3ba-7b82-7240-8e7f-2a8a87a1ad11 hyp-3, 019de3b9-f831-7912-8a80-47b055255a03]: Hypothesis assumes launchagent-dispatched agents can address replies to human:simon. launchagent injects no NOVA_SLUG env var into subagent sessions. nova-server auth requires X-Nova-Slug header to identify sender. A dispatched agent that does not know its own slug cannot authenticate, cannot send, and the reply channel is dead on arrival — not a future concern, a present architecture gap.

MISREAD USER-NEED [attacks 019de3ba-7b82-7240-8e7f-2a8a87a1ad11, 019de3ba-65b2-7853-956d-3f5fdf6fb120]: Nova conflates two distinct problems: (a) Simon receiving async results from dispatched agents, and (b) agent-to-agent handoff in Orokin pipelines. These have different latency tolerances, different auth requirements, and different failure modes. One 'email-shaped' API serves neither well: Simon's case needs push notification, agent handoff needs structured task context — not free-form message bodies.

SIMPLER ALTERNATIVE: The core nova use case — agent writes result, Simon reads it later — is already solved by simaris. Convention: dispatched agent runs simaris add with type=reply, tags=task:<id>,reply. Simon runs simaris search for task:<id> type:reply. Zero new daemons, zero new ports, zero auth surface, zero InMemoryStore fragility. Simaris already has durable SQLite, full-text search, and a link graph. Nova adds 4 crates, a server, an auth layer, threading semantics, and daemon lifecycle overhead to reinvent what simaris already is — this should require explicit justification before a single line ships.

WRONG PROBLEM [attacks README, 019de3ba-4b59-7520-b50a-f31d5f3e448e, 019de3ba-7b82-7240-8e7f-2a8a87a1ad11]: README frames nova as 'decouple agent conversations from harness/host' — a broad messaging infrastructure. The only validated pain point in research is that launchagent is fire-and-forget with no result return path. That is a launchagent output-capture problem, solvable with an --output-slug flag that writes to simaris on completion (~20 lines of shell). Building a full async messaging daemon with threading, branching, auth, and attachment limits to fix a CLI output gap is 10x the surface area for 1x the benefit. Nova should not exist until launchagent+simaris is proven insufficient.

# claude stream-json schema — message types produced by launchagent-tmux

Source: `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose`  
Real example: `/tmp/launchagent-0e27bb3b-535d-4abc-a56c-040ee1c00050.jsonl`  
Companion pretty: `/tmp/launchagent-<sid>.pretty` (task description lives here, NOT in JSONL)

## Envelope fields (all messages)
`type`, `subtype` (nullable), `uuid`, `session_id`

## Message types

### system / init
Fields: `cwd`, `session_id`, `tools[]`, `mcp_servers[]`, `model`, `permissionMode`,
`slash_commands[]`, `apiKeySource`, `claude_code_version`, `output_style`, `agents[]`,
`skills[]`, `plugins[]`, `analytics_disabled`, `uuid`, `memory_paths{}`, `fast_mode_state`
Note: NO `task` field — task string is NOT in JSONL.

### system / task_started
Fields: `task_id`, `tool_use_id`, `description` (shell cmd), `task_type` ("local_bash"), `uuid`, `session_id`

### system / task_notification
Fields: `task_id`, `tool_use_id`, `status` ("completed"), `output_file`, `summary`, `uuid`, `session_id`

### system / task_updated
Fields: `task_id`, `patch{}` (e.g. `{"is_backgrounded": true}`), `uuid`, `session_id`

### system / hook_started
Fields: `hook_id`, `hook_name`, `hook_event` (e.g. "SessionStart"), `uuid`, `session_id`

### system / hook_response
Fields: `hook_id`, `hook_name`, `hook_event`, `output`, `stdout`, `stderr`, `exit_code`, `outcome` ("success"), `uuid`, `session_id`

### assistant
Fields: `message{}`, `parent_tool_use_id` (null for top-level), `session_id`, `uuid`
`message`: `model`, `id`, `type`, `role`, `content[]`, `stop_reason`, `stop_sequence`, `usage{}`, `diagnostics`, `context_management`
Content block types:
- `text` → `text` (string)
- `thinking` → `thinking` (string), `signature` (string)
- `tool_use` → `id`, `name`, `input{}`, `caller{}` (e.g. `{"type":"direct"}`)

### user
Fields: `message{}`, `parent_tool_use_id`, `session_id`, `uuid`, `timestamp`, `tool_use_result{}`
`message.content[]` type:
- `tool_result` → `tool_use_id`, `content` (string or array of `{type,text}`), `is_error`
`tool_use_result`: `stdout`, `stderr`, `interrupted`, `isImage`, `noOutputExpected`

### result
Fields: `subtype` ("success" — only observed value; also possible: "error_max_turns"),
`is_error`, `api_error_status`, `duration_ms`, `duration_api_ms`, `num_turns`,
`result` (final assistant text string), `stop_reason`, `session_id`,
`total_cost_usd`, `usage{}`, `modelUsage{}`, `permission_denials[]`,
`terminal_reason` ("completed"), `fast_mode_state`, `uuid`

### rate_limit_event
Fields: `rate_limit_info{}` (`status`, `resetsAt`, `rateLimitType`, `overageStatus`, `overageResetsAt`, `isUsingOverage`), `uuid`, `session_id`

## Key insight for Banshee
Task description is NOT in the JSONL. It is in the `.pretty` companion file header:
```
launchagent  sid=<sid>  aspect=<aspect>  model=<model>
stream: /tmp/launchagent-<sid>.jsonl
task:   <task text>
─────────────────────
```
Banshee must parse `/tmp/launchagent-<sid>.pretty` to show a human-readable agent name.

# Lotus failure — treated filed atom as message to running agent (2026-05-03)

## What happened
Simaris agent returned a charter proposal asking "confirm or push back". Agent then EXITED. Lotus filed the confirmation as `lotus-reply-simaris-charter-2026-05-03` and treated that as having communicated. Result: standstill. The reply was passive storage; no agent was reading it. User had to tell lotus the company was at standstill.

## Root cause
Confused two patterns:
- ATOMS are pull-mode (agent reads on next session/launch)
- RUNNING AGENTS need either active dispatch OR a reply-channel (nova reply-slug)

Filed atom did neither. It will be read next time simaris is launched, but the launch never happened automatically.

## Pattern
Subagent dispatch via `launchagent` is one-shot. Agent runs → outputs → exits. There is no "in-flight conversation." Replies go through:
- `--reply-slug <slug>` to post into nova inbox (which agent presumably checks), OR
- next `launchagent` invocation that includes the reply context

## Correction (immediate)
- After ANY synchronous launchagent run that ends with the agent asking lotus a question: lotus must EITHER re-launch with the answer embedded OR post via nova reply-slug. Filing a "reply atom" does neither.
- For long-running programs (like the knowledge program), prefer dispatching with explicit "execute, don't ask" framing so the agent makes the call autonomously.

## Devil note
Twice now in one session: lotus has filed atoms believing they were communications. First time was the "no confirmation needed" reply. The pattern suggests lotus's mental model treats simaris-the-store as an async message bus, but it isn't — there are no listeners.

## Procedure update needed
Add a procedure: "After launchagent that ends with question to lotus → re-launch or use reply-slug. Never treat a filed atom as a sent message."
